title: Melkote Temple Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Melkote Temple Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Karnataka

overview.short: Melkote Temple Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area situated in the Mandya district of Karnataka, India.
overview.description: Melkote Temple Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area situated in the Mandya district of Karnataka, India. Established on June 17, 1974, it was primarily created to provide a refuge for the Indian wolf. The sanctuary is named after the historic Melkote pilgrimage town and its prominent temples, which border the protected zone.

topPlaces.name: Advikatte
topPlaces.description: Advikatte is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Nagamangala taluk of Mandya district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Cheluvanarayana Swamy Temple
topPlaces.description: Cheluvanarayana Swamy Temple, also known as Selva Pillai or Thirunarayanapuram, is a temple in Melukote in the Mandya District of Karnataka, India. The temple lies on the foothills of Melkote and is associated with the Yoga Narasimha temple built on rocky hills which overlook the Kaveri valley.

topPlaces.name: Melukote
topPlaces.description: Melukote in Pandavapura taluk of Mandya district, Karnataka, in southern India, is one of the sacred places in Karnataka. It is built on rocky hills, known as Yadugiri, Yaadavagiri or Yadushaila, overlooking the Cauvery valley.

topPlaces.name: Chinya
topPlaces.description: Chinya is a small village in Mandya district of Karnataka state, India.

topPlaces.name: Jakkanahalli
topPlaces.description: Jakkanahalli is a small village in Mandya district of Karnataka state, India.

topPlaces.name: Shingapoore
topPlaces.description: Shingapoore is a small village in the Mandya district of the Indian state of Karnataka. It is located 5 km north of the Narayanapura village, near the town of Pandavapura.

items.note: Advikatte is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Nagamangala taluk of Mandya district in Karnataka.

Cheluvanarayana Swamy Temple, also known as Selva Pillai or Thirunarayanapuram, is a temple in Melukote in the Mandya District of Karnataka, India. The temple lies on the foothills of Melkote and is associated with the Yoga Narasimha temple built on rocky hills which overlook the Kaveri valley.

Melukote in Pandavapura taluk of Mandya district, Karnataka, in southern India, is one of the sacred places in Karnataka. It is built on rocky hills, known as Yadugiri, Yaadavagiri or Yadushaila, overlooking the Cauvery valley.
Chinya is a small village in Mandya district of Karnataka state, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Melkote Temple Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Melkote Temple Wildlife Sanctuary is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Melkote Temple Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mysuru Airport (52 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (43 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Melkote Temple Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Melkote Temple Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Melkote Temple Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Melkote Temple Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Melkote Temple Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Advikatte, Cheluvanarayana Swamy Temple, Melukote, Chinya, Jakkanahalli.
